variant: give me a morning skincare checklist i can actually follow

request.message: give me a morning skincare checklist i can actually follow

response_body.response: here's your AM checklist. keep it simple: cleanser → hydration → active (optional) → moisturizer → SPF. that's it. the mistake most people make is stacking too much. one active at a time, and only if your barrier is solid.  if your skin is reactive or inflamed right now, drop the active entirely and just do cleanser → hydration → moisturizer → SPF until the barrier stabilizes.

products.name: The Ordinary Azelaic Acid Suspension 10%
products.description: Azelaic acid for redness, PIH, and mild rosacea — pregnancy-safe.

products.name: CeraVe PM Facial Moisturizing Lotion
products.description: Lightweight ceramide + niacinamide moisturizer for nightly use.

items: cleanse (removes oil, dirt, sunscreen buildup so actives reach skin)
apply toner or hydrating mist on damp skin (optional, but helps absorption)
active: azelaic acid if acne-prone, or skip if barrier is compromised
treatment serum (centella or panthenol to support skin)
hydrating serum (hyaluronic acid, beta-glucan, or peptides)
moisturizer (ceramides + lipids to lock in hydration and repair barrier)
SPF (non-negotiable; blocks UV damage and pigmentation)
